instruction: Complete each logical job independently and return one keyed result item for that job. Never merge jobs, scenes, owners, routes, or chronologies. A transport batch is not a narrative unit.
security: Use only the structured records in this request. Do not use tools, web, MCP, commands, files, applications, runtime tracing, or outside knowledge.
authority: M10 graph facts, M11 human structure, and M12 route status and prerequisite wording remain authoritative. Do not change their IDs, ownership, membership, chronology, route correctness, status, or wording. M13-only character_ids on an M10 evidence handle are a deterministic participation projection from that exact owned statement; they do not change M11 membership. Clearly distinguish factual claims from interpretation.
evidence: Use only prompt-local evidence or child-claim handles supplied inside the same logical job. Copy handles exactly. Never invent, infer, reuse across jobs, or expand a handle into an authority ID. Scene claims use E handles in evidence_handles and an empty child_claim_handles array. Segment, chapter, route, ending, plot, and character hierarchy claims use C handles in child_claim_handles and an empty evidence_handles array. Never mix E and C support in one claim.
output: Return exactly one JSON object satisfying the response schema. Each output item must repeat exactly one logical_job_id from its own input item. Use content_refusal for an item that cannot be completed; do not refuse valid sibling jobs.

successful_item_payload.title: A concise non-empty title of at most 200 characters.
successful_item_payload.summary: A concise non-empty summary of at most 4000 characters.

claims.text: A non-empty claim of at most 1000 characters.
claims.evidence_handles: Unique prompt-local E handles owned by this scene job, or an empty array for a hierarchy claim.
claims.child_claim_handles: Unique prompt-local C handles owned by this hierarchy job, or an empty array for a scene claim.
claims.subject: A non-empty normalized subject of at most 320 characters.
claims.predicate: A non-empty normalized predicate of at most 320 characters.
claims.normalized_value: A non-empty normalized value of at most 320 characters.

targeted_repair_payload.use_only_when: The logical job input explicitly identifies a targeted repair request. A whole-artifact repair still uses successful_item_payload.
targeted_repair_payload.title: Return a valid corrected title or copy the prior valid title.
targeted_repair_payload.summary: Return a valid corrected summary or copy the prior valid summary.
targeted_repair_payload.claims: Return only requested invalid claim replacements. Each replacement has exact keys ordinal, claim_class, text, evidence_handles, child_claim_handles, subject, predicate, polarity, normalized_value. Ordinal must be one explicitly requested invalid ordinal.
